title: Catalog Change Event
description: A single event emitted by an AdCP agent's catalog change feed (GET /catalog/events). Events are append-only, UUID-v7-ordered, and denormalized — the payload carries the post-change state of the entity so consumers can update local state without a follow-up get_products / get_signals call. The discriminator is `event_type`; each branch defines the payload shape. See specs/catalog-change-feed.md for the full sync semantics and security posture.

event_id.description: UUID **v7** identifier — monotonically ordered without server-clock skew. Consumers use this as the cursor on subsequent /catalog/events polls. The `format: uuid` schema constraint accepts any UUID version; v7 is a spec-level MUST that schema validators will not catch alone — sellers MUST emit v7 specifically, callers SHOULD verify the version nibble when ordering matters.

entity_type.description: Entity class. 'product' for product.* events, 'signal' for signal.* events, 'catalog' for catalog.bulk_change.

entity_id.description: Entity identifier. For product.* events, the product_id. For signal.* events, the signal_agent_segment_id. For catalog.bulk_change, an agent-defined operation id.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the agent emitted the event. Advisory — consumers MUST order by event_id (UUID v7), not by created_at, to avoid clock-skew artifacts.

payload.description: Event-type-specific payload. Shape is determined by event_type per the oneOf below.

oneOf.description: product.created — a new product was added to the agent's catalog.

applies_to.description: REQUIRED. Sellers MUST declare the cache layer explicitly on every *.created event. When introducing an entity that exists only in an account overlay (e.g., a custom product for a single account), the seller MUST emit { scope: 'account', account_ids: [...] } to prevent the entity from leaking into every consumer's public-layer cache. For public-layer additions, declare { scope: 'public' } explicitly rather than relying on a default — schema-required declaration prevents the quiet-failure path where a forgotten applies_to leaks an account-only entity to all consumers.

oneOf.description: product.updated — product metadata changed (name, description, formats, properties, targeting capabilities, measurement terms). Use changed_fields for fine-grained re-render, but be prepared to handle a full replacement.

product.description: Full post-change Product object. RECOMMENDED for denormalized event consumption.

changed_fields.description: Advisory list of changed top-level field names (e.g., ['format_ids', 'performance_standards']). Consumers MAY use for fine-grained re-render but MUST be able to handle a full replacement of the entity.

oneOf.description: product.priced — pricing options changed (new option, removed option, price/floor change). Existing media buys unaffected; composition layer must re-price.

pricing_options.description: Full post-change pricing_options array. NOT a delta — consumers replace the prior options with this list.

previous_pricing_option_ids.description: Optional. Lets consumers detect which pricing_option_ids were retired.

effective_at.description: When the price change takes effect. A value in the future is a pre-announcement: consumers MAY warm caches but MUST NOT bind decisions until the effective time has passed. See specs/catalog-change-feed.md §`effective_at` and pre-announce.

retracts_event_id.description: Optional. When this event retracts a prior pre-announced *.priced (per the §`effective_at` and pre-announce rules), set this to the event_id of the announcement being retracted. Lets consumers detect retraction deterministically by event_id lookup rather than maintaining per-entity 'latest-event' state. When present, the retraction MUST carry effective_at <= the retracted event's effective_at.

oneOf.description: product.removed — product no longer available. In-flight buys honor existing cancellation policy regardless of removal_reason; storefront catalog UX differs by reason.
signal.created — a new signal was added to the agent's catalog.

applies_to.description: REQUIRED. Agents MUST declare the cache layer explicitly on every *.created event. When introducing a signal that exists only in an account overlay (e.g., a custom segment for a single account), the agent MUST emit { scope: 'account', account_ids: [...] } to prevent leak into every consumer's public-layer cache. For public-layer additions, declare { scope: 'public' } explicitly rather than relying on a default — schema-required declaration prevents the quiet-failure path.

oneOf.description: signal.updated — signal metadata changed (description, coverage, deployments).

pricing_options.description: Full post-change pricing_options array. NOT a delta.

retracts_event_id.description: Optional. When this event retracts a prior pre-announced *.priced, set this to the event_id of the announcement being retracted. See product.priced for the full retraction contract.

oneOf.description: catalog.bulk_change — fast-forward event emitted when a single operation affects many entities. Consumers SHOULD re-bootstrap via wholesale enumeration rather than processing per-entity events.

summary.description: Human-readable description of the bulk operation (e.g., 'Q3 2026 rate card refresh').

affected_entity_types.description: Which entity classes the operation touched.

recommendation.description: Advisory recommendation. Consumers MUST stagger their resyncs per the jittered coalescing rule — the recommendation is not a synchronized trigger. Modeled as a single-value enum (not a boolean) so future minor versions can extend the recommendation vocabulary (e.g., cursor_advance, subscribe_to_subscope) without a breaking shape change. Consumers MUST be prepared to ignore unknown recommendation values.

appliesTo.title: Cache layer scope
appliesTo.description: Declares which cache layer this event invalidates. See specs/catalog-change-feed.md §Cache layering and event scoping.

oneOf.description: Public layer — invalidate the public-layer cache for the entity; all account overlays referencing that public version are also stale.

Account overlay — invalidate the named account overlays only; public layer unaffected. The seller MAY omit account_ids when the affected set is competitively sensitive; the per-subscriber scope filter (see API Endpoints §Per-caller scope filtering) ensures each subscriber sees only events for accounts they're authorized for.

account_ids.description: Optional. When present, names the accounts whose overlays are affected. When omitted, subscribers infer that their own principal's overlay is affected (they received the event because the per-subscriber scope filter routed it to them).

removalReason.description: Why the entity was removed. 'withdrawn': seller-initiated, no resubmit path. 'cancellation': resource cancelled, may return. 'expired': time-bounded availability ended (flight ended, seasonal product retired). 'depublication': underlying property depublished (see publisher.adagents_changed in the registry feed). 'policy_takedown': governance-driven removal (AdCP governance agent OR external regulator/legal takedown). In-flight buys honor existing cancellation policy regardless of reason; storefront catalog UX differs.
